name: Oscilloscope
description: This algorithm implements a simple but useful 2-channel oscilloscope.
gui_description: The display shows one or both waveforms according to the display mode.

parameters.name: Trigger type
parameters.description: The type of trigger, to synchronise the display to the waveform.
enum_values: 1 rising - trigger on the rising edge of input 1
1 falling - trigger on the falling edge of input 1
2 rising - trigger on the rising edge of input 2
2 falling - trigger on the falling edge of input 2

parameters.name: Trigger voltage
parameters.description: Set the trigger voltage – the voltage the input signal has to cross to trigger the oscilloscope.

parameters.name: Time range
parameters.description: Sets the time range corresponding to the full width of the display. The special value “Auto” sets the time range to the time between consecutive triggers.

parameters.name: Display mode
parameters.description: Sets which waveforms are displayed and how. In “XY” mode, inputs 1 & 2 are used as the coordinates to draw, instead of using time as the abscissa.

parameters.name: Draw mode
parameters.description: Sets how the waveform signals are drawn.

parameters.name: Vertical scale 1
parameters.description: Sets the vertical scale for channel 1.

parameters.name: Vertical scale 2
parameters.description: Sets the vertical scale for channel 2.

parameters.name: Vertical offset 1
parameters.description: Sets an amount by which to move the display of channel 1 vertically.

parameters.name: Vertical offset 2
parameters.description: Sets an amount by which to move the display of channel 2 vertically.

parameters.name: Disable screensaver
parameters.description: If set, and the oscilloscope custom UI is displayed, the screensaver is prevented from starting. The 0V line is also disabled, to prevent screen burn-in.
